claim_text: However clinical impact is severely limited by the short 2-min 15O half-life17,18,19.

sub_claims.text: The clinical impact of 15O PET for aerobic glycolysis / oxygen-metabolism imaging in brain tumors is severely limited by the short ~2-min half-life of 15O.

evidence.snippet: Our PET measurements of AG and CMRO 2 are technically demanding and require 15 O inhalation and injection which are not easily available at PET centers, whereas reliable alternative MR techniques are still under development.

All subjects underwent one FDG scan (to measure CMRGlu) and two sets of three 15 O scans (CO, H 2 O, and O 2 ) to measure CBV, CBF, and CMRO 2 . ... CBV was measured with a 5 min emission scan beginning 2 min after brief inhalation of 75 mCi of [ 15 O] carbon monoxide... Dynamic scans of 3 min with 35 2 s frames, 6 5 s frames, and 8 10 s frames were acquired after injection of 50 mCi [ 15 O] water in saline or inhalation of 60 mCi of [ 15 O] oxygen...

sub_claims.paper_value: "technically demanding and require 15O inhalation and injection which are not easily available at PET centers" (no explicit mention of the 2-min half-life as the limiting factor)
sub_claims.claim_value: "short 2-min 15O half-life" as the specific mechanism severely limiting clinical impact
sub_claims.nuance: vlassenko2015 acknowledges that clinical 15O PET is technically demanding and not easily available, and its short-scan protocols (40-s frames, 3-min dynamics) reflect the tracer's brief window. But the paper does not name the ~2-min half-life itself as the limiting factor — the manuscript infers the mechanism. The paper is primarily an application of the technique in brain tumors, not a critique of its clinical utility.

remediation.suggested_edit: Consider citing mintun1984 (which explicitly reports the 123-sec 15O half-life and derives its scan protocol accordingly) and/or a review dedicated to 15O clinical limitations as the primary support for the half-life claim; retain vlassenko2015 as evidence that 15O-PET for AG/CMRO2 remains technically demanding and not widely available.

phrasings_tried: 15O short half-life limits clinical use of oxygen PET
why 15O PET of CMRO2 is not routinely clinical
attestation.closest_adjacent: Discussion (L26): 'Our PET measurements of AG and CMRO 2 are technically demanding and require 15 O inhalation and injection which are not easily available at PET centers, whereas reliable alternative MR techniques are still under development.' — acknowledges clinical limitation but attributes it to technical complexity and availability rather than to the half-life per se.
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: The paper does not credit another primary for the half-life figure; it applies the method and notes availability constraints.
attestation.out_of_context_check: Manuscript uses vlassenko2015 in a sentence specifically about the 2-min 15O half-life as a clinical bottleneck; vlassenko2015 itself is a tumor-AG application paper that notes clinical-availability limitations but does not invoke the half-life number as the mechanism. Citation is adjacent but not a direct match for the specific half-life claim — closer to supporting the broader 'clinical impact limited' framing than the half-life specifically.

sibling_verdicts.excerpt: mintun1984 (ingested; see C022): 'physical decay (0-15 half-life = 123 sec)' — directly supports the ~2-min 15O half-life figure; adjacent in the same co-cite cluster.
Not ingested in this run. Co-cite context unassessed.
